rationale: The citing paper uses Mittal et al. (2021) to support a core premise that safety-related performance is a value-relevant supplier attribute in B2B markets, not just a compliance issue. It also cites that paper when defining key supplier-evaluation attributes for the conjoint design (alongside quality, after-sales support, and price), so the citation informs study setup rather than serving as a passing mention. Although the new paper does not reuse the cited paper's asymmetry/heterogeneity modeling, it relies on it as prior empirical grounding for the paper's framing and attribute selection.
relation_description: The 2024 paper treats Mittal et al. (2021) as prior empirical foundation that safety matters for B2B customer value and supplier evaluation. Building from that premise, it provides new causal evidence on safety training effects (hazards and injuries) and links safety training to supplier choice.
